tagline: Plan mode actually works now, and the usage modal surfaces real rate-limit info instead of staying half-blind.

highlights.text: Live reset countdown + status chips on usage

sections.title: Plan-mode "Approve" actually executes the plan
sections.subtitle: Clicking Approve in plan mode no longer leaves the agent stuck.
sections.body:
Until now, approving a plan from the modal would resolve the SDK's `ExitPlanMode` tool but the underlying permission state stayed pinned to **plan**, so the very next tool the agent tried (Write/Edit/Bash) got blocked — looking, from the outside, as if the approval had been denied.

Now approving the plan also **explicitly transitions the agent to `acceptEdits`** (matching how Claude Code itself behaves after plan approval): file edits flow without re-prompting, anything else still asks. "Keep planning" stays — pick that when you want to refine the plan instead of executing it.
sections.techDetails: `ClaudeAgentBackend.respondPlan` now calls `queryHandle.setPermissionMode('acceptEdits')` immediately after resolving the `ExitPlanMode` permission with `behavior: 'allow'`, and emits a mode event so the composer pill in the UI reflects the new state. The "deny" branch was also reworded in English (`"User wants to keep planning. Refine the plan; do not exit plan mode."`) so the model parses the rejection cleanly.

sections.title: Usage modal: real-time countdown + status chips
sections.subtitle: We can't show a "Session 47%" the SDK refuses to send, but we can show everything else.
sections.body:
Anthropic's SDK exposes `rate_limit_info` on every turn. For Team accounts the **`utilization` percentage is omitted server-side** — Claude.ai's own UI gets it through a separate authenticated endpoint we can't reach from inside VS Code. So no, we can't invent a fake %. But we were also ignoring 4 fields that ARE sent. Now you see them:

- **Status chip** per window: *Under limit* / *Approaching limit* / *Over limit* — colored green/amber/red.
- **Threshold bar** when the SDK signals you've crossed 80%/90% (rendered as `≥80%`, honest about its precision).
- **Live reset countdown**: `Resets in 2h 39m` updates every minute, instead of staying frozen at whatever it said when the modal opened.
- **Overage state**: if you're using overage, the reset clock for it; otherwise, the reason it's unavailable (`disabled by org`, `out of credits`, etc.).
sections.techDetails: `RateWindow` in `protocol.ts` gained `status` / `resetsAt` / `surpassedThreshold` / `overage` fields. `ClaudeAgentBackend.rateWindow` populates them from `SDKRateLimitInfo` (see `sdk.d.ts:3527`). The `rate_limit_event` of type `overage` is now folded into the five-hour window's `overage` field instead of being dropped. The webview adds a `useCountdown(resetsAt)` hook ticking once a minute (passive in cost, accurate to 60 s). All values come from the SDK — no inference, no fakes.
